summary: Projecting u onto v finds the 'shadow' of u in the direction of v. The scalar projection is the length of that shadow; the vector projection is the shadow itself, pointing along v.
method: Compute the dot product u·v and the magnitude |v|.
Divide u·v by |v| for the scalar projection (a single number).
Multiply v by (u·v)/|v|² for the vector projection (a vector along v).

example.title: Project onto a horizontal vector
example.find: the vector projection of u onto v
